goal: Diagnose why an MDL clearcoat+anisotropic material lost fidelity on Maya->USD round-trip into Isaac Sim, then rebuild to preserve MDL.
thoughts: Confirm binding resolves before blaming the exporter. Maya USD often drops MDL clearcoat layer and anisotropy tangents flip under Y-up to Z-up. Rebuild MDL in-place rather than falling back to UsdPreviewSurface (art direction requires MDL fidelity). RTX Interactive path-traced for honest look-dev A/B.
code:
prims = list_all_prims(type_filter="Material", path_prefix="/World/Hero")
meta = get_prim_metadata(path="<MATERIAL_PATH>")
# inspect shader id + source asset to see if clearcoat network survived
mat = create_material(
    path="/World/Looks/Hero_Brushed_Aluminum",
    shader="MDL",
    mdl_source="<MDL_MODULE>",
    params={
        "base_color": "<RGB>",
        "roughness": "<FLOAT>",
        "anisotropy": "<FLOAT>",
        "anisotropy_rotation": "<RADIANS>",
        "clearcoat_weight": "<FLOAT>",
        "clearcoat_roughness": "<FLOAT>"
    }
)
assign_material(prim_path="/World/Hero/Body", material_path=mat.path)
set_render_mode(mode="RTXInteractivePathTraced")
capture_viewport(path="<OUT_PNG>", width=1920, height=1080)
failure_modes: Silently substituting UsdPreviewSurface and calling it 'close enough'
Missing the Y-up to Z-up tangent rotation that rotated anisotropy 90 degrees
Claiming MDL clearcoat survived Maya USD export without inspecting the shader network
